summary: The test suite covers core semantic versioning logic well (parse, bump, changelog) with 20 unit tests that exercise happy paths and basic type-based version bumping. However, critical gaps severely limit its completeness: (1) MockFixtures.ps1 is defined but never imported—its commit fixtures, version fixtures, and helper functions (Get-CommitFixture, Get-VersionFixture, Create-MockGitRepo) are unused, wasting valuable test infrastructure; (2) No workflow validation tests—the task explicitly requires testing the GitHub Actions workflow file structure, triggers, actionlint validation, and script references, yet none of this is covered; (3) Limited edge case and error handling—only one error test (invalid semantic version); many boundary cases untested (empty commit arrays, prerelease versions, zero versions, whitespace handling, file permission errors); (4) No real git integration—Create-MockGitRepo fixture exists but isn't used; (5) Test-ThroughAct.ps1 appears disconnected from the main Pester suite. The tests are well-organized with proper BeforeEach/AfterEach cleanup and clear assertions, but the design fails to leverage its own fixtures and omits required workflow validation, making it incomplete for production use.
